title: polypogon monspeliensis subsp. maritimus
intro: Polypogon monspeliensis subsp. maritimus, also known as sea bent or maritime feather grass, is a subspecies of the Polypogon monspeliensis plant, which is a perennial grass native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of sea bent include its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing, as well as its potential to treat digestive issues and respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of sea bent may include infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
